text:
There will always be something quaint about the naturally janky sound of sentence mixing, something that makes it intrinsically funnier than had the mixed words been said in an unmixed fashion. Its unlike a videogame - you want the jank, the jank has a lovely and irreplaceable charm to it. Something about knowing that what was said wasn't actually said as it was, and it only came to be because some silly dude on the web minced and diced audio files into an irreverent patchwork that was never meant to be, but now is for the sole purpose of "because it was funny to make it that way," is what makes sentence mixing so special and irreplaceable by AI voice generation.

Thank you, CS, for still being the silly goober you are after all these years!

text:
The world's understanding of quantum physics has long been a source of frustration for me. QM's combination of being really philosophically and existentially blursed and also requiring a fuckload of advanced math to dig into (not understand, mind you - dig into) has led to various sources coming up various explanations for various quantum phenomena ranging from "Eh, close enough," to, "Are we even talking about the same subject?" And I think that's a real damn shame because all that bullshit does a fan-fucking-tastic job at obscuring the true elegance and genius of what might be modern physics' greatest achievement.

With this video, you have realized my longtime dream: a video that displays the brilliance of Quantum Physics in its truest form.

text:
@keijijohnson9754  Using AI instead of sentence mixing makes it no longer be a YTP.
The whole point of a YTP is that you take source material and edit it into something new using existing footage. Said limitations breed creativity and make YTP what it is today.
If you instead just generate new pieces of audio using an indecipherable algorithm, you did nothing but write a new line for the person to say. The entire "editing existing footage" aspect is removed. It's the lowest effort possible.
Can some videos with AI voices be funny? Sure, although a lot of them end up being slop. Are these videos proper YTPs? No!
